title: picea glauca var. albertiana
intro: Picea glauca var. albertiana, also known as Alberta spruce, is a variety of the white spruce tree native to North America, characterized by its narrow, conical shape and blue-green needles. The medicinal properties of Alberta spruce are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Alberta spruce include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Traditional herbal preparations include steam inhalation, topical applications, and tea infusions, often used to relieve congestion and promote relaxation.
